jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, business, economics, or a related analytical field (e.g., engineering, math, computer science)
- 5+ years of finance experience, or Master’s degree and 3+ years of finance experience
jobs.description:
The Mechatronics and Sustainable Packaging (MSP) team owns Amazon's asin sortation strategy, delivering technologies that improve Amazon’s cost to serve.

The MSP finance team is seeking an analytical, driven, and creative finance leader to support the Sortation Automation Organization, which designs and implements asin sortation automation solutions for Amazon Operations. This position will partner with business teams to design and implement solutions to scale the program across new platforms, networks, and products, while ensuring the financial controls are in place to measure the end-to-end impact on Amazon’s cost to serve.

When not dedicated to forecasting or monthly financial reporting activities, this role spends their time looking for opportunities to improve forecasting accuracy, financial controls, data quality, and cost modeling. This is an exciting role for someone who enjoys influencing both strategy and tactical execution and has a passion for innovation and scale.

To be successful in this role, the incumbent will need excellent communication skills, strong ownership, and the ability to work with complex data sets to build financial analyses that will be used to inform MSP’s strategy and drive engagement across cross-functional stakeholders. This position comes with executive-level visibility, and experience on this team can open up several career opportunities across Amazon.


Key job responsibilities
- Partnering with senior leaders in Worldwide Operations and WW Ops Finance.
- Creating, improving, and maintaining financial analysis (ROI and NPV models) driving operational decisions across WW Operations Fulfillment network.
- Gathering analytical insights and developing financial frameworks to help grow profitability and expand our reach in global environment.
- Driving the core financial processes for the business including Annual Operating Plans, 3 year Strategic Plan, Long Range Plan and Monthly/Quarterly Business Reviews.
- Completing cost / benefit analyses and communicate key drivers / inputs to support business decisions.
- Performing financial reporting, deep project analyses, and financial modeling.
- Facilitating financial review meetings with business groups and working with business managers to ensure effective finance and cost management.
- Creating, improving, and maintaining financial metrics, reports and scorecards.
- Communicating financial and business results including variance analyses to senior business leaders.
- Leading strategic projects.

jobs.description:
Are you passionate about enabling millions of customers around the world to buy anything, anywhere, anytime? Dealing with large scale distributed systems and orchestrating the entire retail ordering workflow?

Amazon's Ordering tech hub is now expanding to Hyderabad. This powerhouse is set to build the next generation of core ordering systems, including refund intelligence, shaping the entire post-click journey.

About the team
Ordering stands at the heart of Amazon retail, guiding the journey from purchase to delivery to resolution, and this new site will play a pivotal role in its evolution. We, the Ordering team, need founding members of software development engineers team, able to dive deep into abstract problems and come up with innovative designs to help us continuously make our platform ready for our current and future challenges.


You are the ideal candidate if you are capable of breaking down and solving complex problems, are customer obsessed, and have sharp business acumen to build a product vision and roadmap that contributes to the success of both our internal and external customers. You are a technical leader with a track record of building and growing strong teams. You thrive in a fast-moving environment where you are able to juggle complex dependencies and requirements while producing optimal solutions. You are passionate about what you do.

Key job responsibilities
* Building, leading and growing diverse, inclusive and high-performing engineering product teams, including recruiting, mentoring, motivating, promoting and performance management.
* Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams including customers, product managers, stakeholders and leaders to ensure the successful delivery of product capabilities.
* Timely execution and prioritization of goals for the team and effectively mitigating risks.
Strategic leadership to create product roadmaps, set the vision for the team and collaboratively partner with other stakeholder teams.
* Technical leadership including architecture and design discussions for science and engineering problems and technical deep dives to ensure resolution of root causes of complex issues.

jobs.description:
Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering (RME) team is central to Amazon's commitment to innovation. As Amazon evolves and adapts, this team makes sure that the tools and technologies we use do as well. As a Senior RME Technician, you'll help us stay one step ahead, adopting the latest technologies and identifying new and efficient ways of working. In this role, you’ll pay close attention to our processes to help maintain our high standards, and you’ll put in place upgrades to take that standard even higher.



Key job responsibilities
- Demonstrate best practice on using equipment safely and monitor teams to ensure everyone follows the correct processes
- Keep track of equipment performance through visual checks, condition-based monitoring, and preventative maintenance tasks and schedule additional servicing when required
- Supervise technicians on shift to support their development and act as the first point of contact for Reliability Maintenance Engineers
- Solve issues in equipment to reduce downtime for operations so they can process packages as quickly as possible
- Support in finding ways to continually improve systems and standardise processes across the EU network

A day in the life
As a Senior RME Technician at BHX10, you’ll lead a team ensuring our Merch on Demand production equipment—such as Kornit printers, fixation systems and heat presses—runs safely and reliably. You’ll coordinate preventative maintenance, support rapid troubleshooting and ensure compliance with safety procedures including LOTO, risk assessments and VSOPs. Using operational data and system monitoring tools, you’ll identify opportunities to improve equipment reliability, quality and efficiency. Working closely with operations and your RME Manager, you’ll help implement improvements across the site. This role operates on shift patterns, including nights and weekends, with strong opportunities for progression within Amazon RME.

About the team
Our Reliability Maintenance Engineering (RME) team keeps the equipment at BHX10 performing at its best. We’re a technically skilled team of engineers and technicians who work together to maintain, troubleshoot and repair the specialised production equipment that powers Amazon’s Merch on Demand business.

From DTG printers and fixation systems to heat presses and supporting automation, we ensure our equipment operates safely, reliably and efficiently. The team also plays a key role in maintaining site compliance and implementing improvements that increase uptime and performance.

Our focus is reducing downtime so customers receive high-quality products on time. Working across shift patterns, we continuously identify opportunities to improve processes, introduce new technology and drive innovation across the network.

- Active and unrestricted Pharmacist License in domicile state

jobs.description:
Innovation is at the core of what we do. We believe that by removing and reducing the barriers that prevent people from taking their medications, we can help customers conveniently get the medications they need, when they need them and take them as prescribed. We provide customers with the ability to find transparent and simple pricing, receive 24/7 customer service support, and have their medications delivered to their doorsteps while creating programs and products that embody our mission and position ourselves in becoming the world’s safest and fastest online pharmacy. We are looking for a Pharmacist Team Lead to join us on our journey to make it drastically easier for customers to find, choose, afford, and engage with the services, products, and professionals they need to get and stay healthy!

We are looking for a Team Lead (Pharmacist) to join us on our journey to make it drastically easier for customers to find, choose, afford, and engage with the services, products, and professionals they need to get and stay healthy!
#everydaybetter

** Applicants must possess state-specific license requirements based on their location: residents of Idaho must hold a valid ID license, residents of Arizona must hold a valid AZ license, residents of New Hampshire must hold a valid NH license, and residents of Massachusetts must possess both NH and MA licenses.

Key job responsibilities
● Management and oversight of a team of pharmacists and technicians who work in the department.
● Coordinate with Operations Managers to provide clinical guidance to process and project changes in coordination with the Pharmacist in Charge (PIC)
● Manage and develop quality improvement measures, including identifying customer issues and implementing process improvement solutions. Answer questions, provide guidance where necessary surrounding department processes
● Work directly with Operations Managers, Compliance team, and Regulatory Operations to ensure adherence to regulatory requirements and maintain compliance standards in daily operations. Monitor and manage daily operational metrics, making real-time decisions on workflow adjustments and resource allocation to maintain service level agreements (SLAs).
● Responsible for the training and development of the team
● Create and manage rotational job assignments and coordinate daily/weekly scheduling to ensure optimal pharmacy team coverage and workflow efficiency
● Investigate quality errors and facilitate proper resolution, reporting, and coaching as necessary
● Collaborate with HR and department leadership team to coach team members accordingly for performance, quality, and attendance concerns when appropriate including proper documentation.
● Work hands-on in production environment to complete operational tasks and escalations that require leadership.
● Ensure timecard accuracy for team prior to payroll being completed
● Preform other duties as assigned by manager

jobs.description:
The AWS Infrastructure Finance team provides end-to-end financial support, driving AWS to be the lowest cost provider in Cloud computing and leader in customer experience. We are looking for a Senior Financial Analyst to join our AWS Energy Finance team. The AWS Energy team is responsible for (1) Delivering Energy to AWS Data Centers, (2) Procuring Renewable Energy to support our 100% Renewable Energy goal, and (3) Developing our Sustainability and Carbon Strategy. This team provides critical support in the form of financial valuation, investment decisions, and driving the global portfolio strategy for Amazon’s Energy and Renewable Energy portfolio.

We are looking for a Sr. Financial Analyst to support the end-to-end energy portfolio: financial planning and analysis (FP&A) reporting, energy opex and capex portfolio reporting, Month End Close processes, automation support, and more. Finance provides critical valuation support, thought leadership, financial acumen and controllership, and aids in critical decision making both at the deal and portfolio level. Our team owns the financial controllership processes and FP&A reporting and consolidation that scale with the portfolio’s growth. This includes utilities opex and energy market rate forecasting, energy and water capex forecasting, new region energy rate forecast, and partnership with the stakeholder teams (business teams, accounting, and technical product teams). Our team has invested in automation functionality for month end close and financial planning process, and we continue integrating our tools and systems to allow for end-to-end automation of our portfolio reporting.

Key job responsibilities
In this role, you can expect to:
- Drive the energy market forecasting across global markets, supporting our AWS energy portfolio
- Present energy market rate quarterly forecast recommendations to Energy business partners and finance partners, developing a deep knowledge of the global energy market
- Report on the financial performance of our Energy Portfolio and assist the preparation of quarterly and annual reviews
- Partner with energy data vendors to source data for our Energy portfolio (Utilities OpEx)
- Complete Month End Close reporting for Utilities OpEx, including writing commentary on rate and volume variance drivers
- Complete Accruals for Utility OpEx based on actual energy market rates and invoiced amounts each month

About the team
In AWS Finance, you will work with exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people who believe financial partnership is critical to customer success. We benefit from diverse experiences and perspectives and are committed to inclusion as a business advantage and the right thing to do. The growth of AWS and scope of Amazon’s business create opportunities to grow your career and broaden experience in any direction you choose. As a pioneer in cloud services, AWS lets you apply data, creativity, and analytical ability to completely new challenges and opportunities. This is a chance to work hard, have fun, and make history.

Amazon tops the 2018 Harris Poll for most reputable company, scoring first for Emotional Appeal, Products & Services, and Financial Performance, and top ten for Vision & Leadership and Workplace Environment. Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the market leader in cloud infrastructure and services, supporting hundreds of thousands of customers of all sizes.

jobs.description:
In Amazon Air, process driven techniques help us deliver smiles to both our internal and external customers daily. There are multiple ways to problem solve here, and we do it with consideration and respect for each other. We are diverse and united. Your thoughts, ideas, and contributions are highly valued here to solve any problem that we may encounter. There is no limit to how high you can “soar” here at Amazon Air!

Amazon Air (AIR) seeks a Supply Chain Manager (SCM) who will help execute short term operations planning for the AIR network. Responsibilities include optimizing for core metrics (cost, speed, volume, and quality), scaling through automation, and collaborating closely with operations and process engineering partners to resolve feasibility issues. All of these require an individual who navigates ambiguous scenarios including decision making without all the required information.

The individual must deliver results and scale despite constrained resources by inventing and simplifying. The SCM must innovate, take risks, and optimize on a network-wide basis. This role offers a great opportunity to learn multiple aspects of Amazon's business, engaging across the broader network.

Key job responsibilities
- Drive network optimization with a data based approach
- Foster relationships with internal business teams: operations, process engineering, customer experience, and labor planning
- Work closely with business intelligence partners to develop tools for scaling/automation of network configuration
- Work within time constraints to meet business critical needs (often managing multiple, time-sensitive and high priority initiatives simultaneously), while measuring and identifying activities performed and ensuring service requirements are met
- Analyze data from numerous databases (using Excel, Access, SQL, and/or other data management systems) and perform ad hoc reporting and analysis as required
- Travel domestically up to 15% of the time to various facilities in the Amazon network

A day in the life
Your day will be a blend of data-driven decision-making, cross-functional collaboration, and innovative problem-solving. You'll dive into network configuration challenges, develop scalable automation tools, and work closely with operations, process engineering, and business intelligence teams to drive transformative solutions.

About the team
We are a diverse, united group of professionals dedicated to delivering exceptional logistics experiences. Our team values creativity, respect, and continuous improvement. We believe in empowering each team member to contribute unique perspectives and solve complex challenges that impact our global operations.
jobs.description_short: In Amazon Air, process driven techniques help us deliver smiles to both our internal and external customers daily. There are multiple ways to problem solve here, and we do it with consideration and respect

jobs.description:
The AWS EC2 Capacity Optimization team is responsible for growing the world's largest Cloud. We manage EC2's online capacity, build inventory replenishment system, and drive utilization improvement for all AWS services.

What we own:
Finding out the optimal tradeoff between AWS service availability and fleet utilization.
Driving fleet utilization improvement where each 1% means millions of additional free cash flow.
Automating tactical and strategic capacity planning tools to optimize for service availability and infrastructure cost.

What you will learn:
Resource management and admission control for the Cloud.
Inventory management and supply chain management for the Cloud.
The internals of all AWS services.
How to build large scale distributed systems for both internal and external AWS customers.

- 5+ years of product or program management, product marketing, business development or technology experience
- Bachelor's degree or equivalent
- Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
- Experience with end to end product delivery
- Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product
- Experience as a product manager or owner
- Experience owning technology products
jobs.description:
The Recruiting Operations Center (ROC) is seeking a Senior Product Manager (Non-Technical) to own and drive the vision, strategy, and delivery of ideal-state process and technical solutions that enable global Amazon University Talent Acquisition (AUTA) hiring.

This role serves as the strategic business owner for a specific stage of the AUTA recruitment lifecycle, partnering closely with recruiting teams, business customers, and technical teams to define and champion ideal-state workflows that identify where process and technology changes are needed. You will work with these seem stakeholders to translate these visions into clear use cases, prioritized requirements, and scalable solutions — while ensuring the needs of multiple personas (business customers, candidates, and recruiters) remain at the center of every decision.

You will shape roadmap direction and influence delivery by providing data-driven business context, ensuring AUTA's specific needs are met, while proactively identifying and collaborating on solutions that could benefit all of Amazon recruitment. While you do not directly own technical product delivery, you are accountable for driving the lifecycle forward at every stage — and through that accountability, responsible for delivering the right outcomes. You will develop your voice as a customer by actively gathering and synthesizing inputs from recruiters, candidates, business stakeholders, and subject-matter experts, ensuring the needs of all personas are represented in every decision. You will ensure the vision is defined, the right business cases are built, and that delivery stays anchored to those needs, even when the work is carried out by others. Your responsibilities span the full product lifecycle — from vision definition and business case development through metrics monitoring and change management — requiring both strategic thinking and operational excellence. You will guide initiatives from early discovery through launch readiness, balancing immediate business needs with long-term operational scalability.

Success in this role requires exceptional written and verbal communication skills, strong influence, and structured thinking. You must be able to craft compelling narratives for senior leadership and stakeholders, facilitate complex discussions across diverse stakeholder groups, and drive clarity across multiple product teams without direct authority. You should be comfortable operating in ambiguity, translating complex business needs into actionable requirements, maintaining momentum across interdependent workstreams, and balancing competing priorities while keeping the needs of multiple customer personas at the center of every decision.

Key job responsibilities
Define and champion the product vision for AUTA experience domains, working across technical, program, and recruiting teams to align on a shared direction that serves the full range of personas — business customers, candidates, and recruiters.

Develop your voice as a customer by actively gathering and synthesizing inputs from customers and subject-matter experts.

Using those insights, act as the business owner for AUTA product and process domains — designing ideal-state workflows, standardizing processes, and translating diverse needs into clear business use cases, prioritized technical requirements, and compelling narratives for senior leadership.

Use best practices and mechanisms to keep stakeholders aligned on expectations, progress, and delivery timelines; drive crisp decisions, clear blockers, and escalate appropriately to maintain momentum.

Define, monitor, and ensure solutions improve key success metrics (KPIs); apply rigorous analytics to identify anomalies and ensure decisions reflect the full customer population.

Develop and execute change management strategies, including SOPs, operational readiness materials, and adoption plans to support successful rollout across multiple teams and personas
Actively contribute to recruiting, mentoring, and developing talent across the team

A day in the life
In this role, you will work across technical, program, and recruitment teams, serving as the connective tissue between AUTA business needs and technical solution delivery. On a typical day, you may partner with technical product managers to translate non-technical business use-cases into technical requirements and clarify business needs across multiple user personas, drive intake and prioritization discussions across competing initiatives, review designs and workflows to ensure the needs of all customer segments are fully represented, analyze performance metrics to identify optimization opportunities, and coordinate launch readiness and change management planning.

jobs.description:
Amazon Air (AIR) is looking for a Senior Program Manager (Sr. PM) with excellent communication skills (verbal and written) and a record of delivering results who will own and execute strategic, ambiguous, and cross-functional projects across the Global Strategic Programs and Initiatives (GSPI) organization. The team is comprised of leaders who are passionate about building the roadmap to enable AIR to scale the business and drive innovation through four main components/pillars: operational excellence and rhythm of business processes, standardization mechanisms, exploratory projects, and strategic program creation/management.

The Sr. PM will enable implementation of Program Management Office (PMO) best practices, scope and create large projects, track project timelines, and develop mechanisms for communicating to senior management on status, risks, and business challenges. The Sr. PM will own implementing and governing a common set of PMO processes and supporting tools for managing the AIR portfolio of high visibility programs to enable efficient operating rhythm for the organization, including: managing executive business reviews, cross-team coordination, and executive presentation.

This role requires strong program management fundamentals, but differs from traditional PM roles. It is essential for this Sr. PM to operate comfortably in ambiguity, be able to develop solutions and execute on them independently, and seamlessly move between strategic and tactical work. We are looking for a self-starter who can build structure where none exists, communicate effectively (written and verbal), and build strong relationships to Earn Trust across executive leaders and multiple stakeholder groups. If you seek this fast-paced Day One environment, come work on the AIR team!


Key job responsibilities
- Manage cross-functional teams, and plan/organize/execute complex projects/programs across multiple organizations (Operations, Tech, Finance, Third Party Vendors, etc.) to enable the launch of new products, tools, and processes.
- Partner with key functional stakeholders across the company to lead, support, and influence new business initiatives.
- Build strong partnerships with operational leaders to identify the impact of new business initiatives.
- Innovate to improve project scope/execution and improve customer outcomes while decreasing the end-to-end supply chain cost.
- Escalate risks in the project plan to the relevant owners and teams.
- Navigate through ambiguity.
- Manage and coordinate multiple project assignments simultaneously in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.
- Track end-to-end project status and incorporate business as usual transition mechanisms.
- Own the planning, organization, and execution of complex projects across multiple organizations and stakeholders, including development of Program Management Office (PMO) processes.
- Own multiple business narrative reports (white papers), simultaneously.
- Create and build new tools, processes, and mechanisms with both Tech and Non-Tech resources.
- Potential to travel 10% per year.

About the team
The Global Strategic Programs and Initiatives (GSPI) team is comprised of leaders who work in a highly ambiguous and autonomous environment. GSPI strives to deliver results within four main components/pillars: operational excellence and rhythm of business processes, standardization mechanisms, exploratory projects, and strategic program creation/management.

jobs.company_name: Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V. - D44
jobs.description:
This position is a key leadership role within the Mexican Engineering team with responsibility for the Mexico Amazon Customer Fulfillment (CF) & Transportation Services (ATS) region. The successful candidate will be an experienced Engineering Manager with proven Project Management skills, demonstrated leadership of remote teams across multiple locations, and a track record of navigating complex situations and dealing effectively with ambiguity. This role is responsible for providing a safe working environment for Amazon associates and all aspects of engineering infrastructure, statutory compliance, preventative and reactive maintenance at specific nodes of our CF and ATS.
The person will be a role model in setting a high bar for performance within their team, supported by clear goals for each team member. A strong and engaging people manager with exceptional communication skills; this person must be capable of leading and inspiring remote teams, ensuring alignment and engagement despite geographical distances. They will understand that success is delivered when the Team is fully engaged on common goals and that success is measured by internal and external customer satisfaction. The ideal candidate will be an outstanding communicator who can effectively influence stakeholders at all levels, articulate complex technical concepts clearly, and build strong relationships across distributed teams. This position requires an Engineering professional who is a dynamic, high-energy, results-oriented individual with both the stamina and mental agility to be able to work and thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth, innovative, customer-centric environment.

Key Accountabilities

Strategic

- Contribute to the development and implementation of Amazon's growth in the region/pillar.
- Responsible for the formulation, adherence and tracking of RME capital and operational expenditure (CAPEX and OPEX) across the region. Deliver the required availability of all infrastructures in their region to meet customer demand whilst remaining within operational budget.
- Engage and work closely with the regions strategic leaders in Operations, Transportation, Finance and Supply Chain to ensure an integrated approach is adopted that underpins growth and which delivers customer service excellence.
- Drive a culture of innovation in the team which will lead to improvements in safety, customer experience and system performance.

Technical

- Responsible for the overall building and facility maintenance (Reliability, Performance, Cost, etc.).
- Responsible for all preventative and reactive maintenance for installed equipment in the region, ensuring maximum equipment availability (Conveyors, Sorters, Labeling Machines, Packing Machine, HVAC, Fire detection, etc.).
- Drive and improve equipment and process standardization across the region and the wider network.
- Provide feedback and condition analysis on equipment, performance and availability.
- Manage day to day issues on equipment with the regional Engineering teams, defining priorities with the Operations teams and working with the local teams to resolve TTs promptly and with detailed root cause and remedy.
- Support the Ops Engineering teams with installation of new Equipment.
- Effective in influencing, negotiating and communicating with internal and external business partners, contractors and vendors.
- Provide technical guidance to field personnel.
- Experience in Lean Manufacturing System.
- An ability to create clear, concise narratives with supporting financial justification for capital investment and then delivery of complex technical solutions.

Leadership Attributes

- Provide leadership, direction, support and coaching to your direct reports, ensuring that they are fully engaged in delivering against the common, SMART goals and that they are developed to the limit of their potential. A key barometer of success in the leadership of the team is the creation and constant review of a succession plan.
- Excel at remote leadership, utilizing strong communication skills to maintain team cohesion, drive accountability, and foster collaboration across geographically dispersed teams.
- Through positive and visible leadership, develop a culture of challenge, continuous improvement and engagement to establish a right first time capability.
- Continue to build on the philosophy that "the customer needs come first and work back from there". Always ensure that the team is empowered to influence decisions that impact on customer experience.
- Able to switch from operating at a strategic level or a hands on level and understand when each is the appropriate approach to support the Team. Ability to function, think clearly and precisely to find the right solution in a fast-paced work environment.



About the team
Inclusive team culture
At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.
 
Work Life Balance
We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.
 
Mentorship & Career Growth
Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.
